title: Roseanne's Return

jokes.text: Hank arriving late to a meeting about keeping the show fresh with 'hey now... oh, started on time'
jokes.setup: Staff meeting about preventing staleness and malaise on the show
jokes.punchline: The sidekick who embodies staleness arrives late with his signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Hank's obliviousness to his own contribution to show staleness, delivered with his trademark lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: hey now... oh, started on time

jokes.text: Kato kaelin has people?
jokes.setup: Paula mentions Kato Kaelin's people keep calling
jokes.punchline: Incredulous question highlighting how minor celebrities have representation
jokes.explanation: Sharp industry observation about celebrity culture and representation

dialogue.line: Kato kaelin has people?

jokes.text: Hank's property values complaint about living on Rockingham
jokes.setup: Discussion about O.J. trial victims
jokes.punchline: Hank makes it about his property values instead of actual murder victims
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Hank's narcissism turning tragedy into personal inconvenience

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Hank's property values complaint about living on Rockingham

jokes.text: you bumped him for faith ford. Hey, who knew he was going to get this hot?
jokes.setup: Discussion about O.J.'s previous show appearance
jokes.punchline: Treating murder trial like celebrity buzz/heat

dialogue.line: you bumped him for faith ford.
Hey, who knew he was going to get this hot?

jokes.text: This is just what we needed. A sense of direction. Here we go. Hank.

jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan sarcasm about this being exactly what they needed

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This is just what we needed. A sense of direction. Here we go. Hank.

jokes.text: Well, aren't you a goddamn smart fellow because it was at planet fucking Hollywood. I mean who wants to eat buffalo wings sitting next to demi Moore's torn panties from disclosure?

dialogue.line: Well, aren't you a goddamn smart fellow because it was at planet fucking Hollywood. I mean who wants to eat buffalo wings sitting next to demi Moore's torn panties from disclosure?

jokes.text: Oh, stop. I smell sulfur.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, stop. I smell sulfur.

jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay but perfectly in character for Artie's clumsy attempts at being smooth

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: She puts the supe in super model.

jokes.text: because when they kill tuna, they kill dolphins, and dolphins are people. People of the sea.

dialogue.speaker: Ivanka
dialogue.line: because when they kill tuna, they kill dolphins, and dolphins are people. People of the sea.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: They're fish, they're not people. Fish. The fight ends in fish.

dialogue.line: The lingerie catalog fucked you?

jokes.text: Oh, fuck.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, fuck.

jokes.text: Do svidaniya.

dialogue.speaker: Ivanka
dialogue.line: Do svidaniya.

jokes.text: Larry, you asshole, I know you're there. It's roseanne. Call me if you have the balls.

dialogue.speaker: Roseanne
dialogue.line: Larry, you asshole, I know you're there. It's roseanne. Call me if you have the balls.

dialogue.line: Harrison ford, Steve Martin, Michelle pfeiffer. Who else?

jokes.text: Are you Larry Sanders? No.

dialogue.speaker: Fan
dialogue.line: Are you Larry Sanders?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: What brings you here? The show get cancelled? No, no. No, not yet.
jokes.setup: Larry meeting Chevy in therapy waiting room
jokes.punchline: Chevy's casual assumption about show cancellation
jokes.explanation: Good insider humor - casual cruelty of showbiz assumptions

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What brings you here?

dialogue.speaker: Chevy
dialogue.line: The show get cancelled?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, no. No, not yet.

jokes.text: I thought it was 19 months. On the 12th. Right.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Chevy's show cancellation
jokes.punchline: Larry correcting the timeline of Chevy's failure
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable precision - Larry's competitive need to be accurate about others' failures

dialogue.line: I thought it was 19 months.
On the 12th.
Right.

jokes.text: I had a fish behind my head... everybody said, 'fish? Why fish?' I don't know. Why fish?

jokes.text: Hello, mayor. Hank Kingsley. Listen, a little problem. Easy fix.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Hello, mayor. Hank Kingsley. Listen, a little problem. Easy fix.

jokes.text: You could say that, uh, you live on rockingham, just a knife's throw from o.j.'s house. Uh, no. I don't think I could do that one. That... that... that implies o.j. Is guilty.

jokes.punchline: Hank's moral concern about implying guilt
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy - the knife joke is perfect, Hank's moral stance absurdly selective

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: You could say that, uh, you live on rockingham, just a knife's throw from o.j.'s house.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Uh, no. I don't think I could do that one. That... that... that implies o.j. Is guilty.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sure if you looked at all my underwear you'd find some blood there, too.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I'm... I'm going to go out and murder someone without my shoes on. Come on, come on.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'm... I'm going to go out and murder someone without my shoes on. Come on, come on.

jokes.text: He's always been nice to me.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He's always been nice to me.

jokes.text: if o.j. Had really just admitted he was guilty from the get-go, he'd be out by now.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: if o.j. Had really just admitted he was guilty from the get-go, he'd be out by now.

jokes.text: The latest DNA study now shows o.j. Didn't even gain those 2,000 yards that year.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The latest DNA study now shows o.j. Didn't even gain those 2,000 yards that year.

tmdb_overview: Larry is apprehensive over a guest appearance by former girl friend Roseanne and decides to see a psychiatrist. The office follows the O.J. trial.
